page: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2048 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding a high resource density of 32 GB of RAM per physical core. It is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core processing and cryptographic performance. The instance achieved a passmark CPU score of 56826.42 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 61947.26, both placing in the top 25 percent of tested systems. Database operations are highly efficient, with a Passmark database score of 26144.88. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 3352.15 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 153.34 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, while single-core performance and memory scores align with average tier benchmarks.

Given the massive memory-to-vCPU ratio and integrated high-speed local NVMe storage, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound enterprise workloads by reducing the need to scale out compute nodes solely for memory capacity. The hardware profile is optimized for running large-scale in-memory databases, heavy database operations, software compilation, and data-intensive enterprise applications. It is also capable of handling medium-scale LLM inference tasks, as demonstrated by strong text generation throughput on smaller models.
description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for data-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores running at 3.5 GHz, the server features 2048 GB of DDR4 RAM, providing an exceptional 32 GB of memory per core. It includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 50 Gbps baseline network interface, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core CPU performance, high database operations throughput, and efficient software compilation capabilities. Single-core metrics and memory bandwidth align with mid-range standards. This configuration is highly suited for large in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and memory-bound data processing tasks where high memory density and local storage speed are critical.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 2048 GB DDR4 RAM, and 1900 GB NVMe SSD storage, designed for large-scale database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.16xlarge features 64 Intel vCPUs, 2048 GB RAM, and 1900 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring massive DDR4 capacity, dedicated Intel Xeon processors, and fast local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
2048 GB of DDR4 memory providing 32 GB of RAM per core
1900 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Optimized for in-memory databases and data-intensive enterprise applications
